sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Wolverhampton are in poor form with 3 consecutive losses in their last 5 matches, conceding multiple goals in recent games. Brentford, on the other hand, have shown attacking prowess, winning 3 of their last 5 matches and scoring consistently, with key players like Mbeumo and Wissa in excellent form.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Brentford have dominated, winning both with an aggregate score of 7-3. Both games saw high-scoring encounters, with Brentford often striking first and Wolverhampton failing to keep a clean sheet in 5 consecutive meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Brentford rank higher in the league (9th vs Wolverhampton’s 14th) and have a better goal-scoring record (65 vs 53). Wolverhampton struggle defensively, conceding 68 goals (17th in the league), while Brentford's attack has been clinical, supported by their higher big chances created and conversion rates.

sections.subtitle: Key Players Impact
sections.text: Matheus Cunha has been Wolverhampton’s standout with recent goals and assists, but Brentford’s attacking duo of Mbeumo and Wissa, both with high Sofascore ratings and goal contributions, pose a significant threat. Brentford’s midfield creativity, led by Damsgaard, could exploit Wolves’ defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Game Dynamics
sections.text: Given Brentford’s tendency to score early (often in the first half) and Wolverhampton’s recent defensive struggles, expect Brentford to take the lead. Wolves may respond with late efforts, as seen in their goal distribution, but Brentford’s form suggests they can hold or extend their advantage.
